mof2text: C288H1344Cu48O720 has unit-cell lengths of 26.399 Å, 26.399 Å, and 26.399 Å with cell angles of 90.0°, 90.0°, and 90.0°, and crystallizes in the F432 space group. This non-interpenetrated 3d framework with the tbo topology, it contains one type of inorganic building block, one type of organic building block, 32 free molecules (H12O3) and 192 free molecules (H4O2). For 24 building blocks 1 (C4H4Cu2O10): Cu(1) is bonded in a square pyramidal geometry to one O(2) and four equivalent O(1) atoms. The Cu(1)-O(2) bond length is 2.13 Å. All Cu(1)-O(1) bond lengths are 1.97 Å. C(1) is bonded in a bent 120 degrees geometry to two equivalent O(1) atoms. Both C(1)-O(1) bond lengths are 1.25 Å. H(1) is bonded in a single-bond geometry to one O(2) atom. The H(1)-O(2) bond length is 0.92 Å. There are two inequivalent O sites. In the first O site, O(1) is bonded in a single-bond geometry to one Cu(1) and one C(1) atom. In the second O site, O(2) is bonded in a trigonal planar geometry to one Cu(1) and two equivalent H(1) atoms. For 32 building blocks 2 (C6H3): There are two inequivalent C sites. In the first C site, C(1) is bonded in a bent 120 degrees geometry to two equivalent C(2) atoms. Both C(1)-C(2) bond lengths are 1.38 Å. In the second C site, C(2) is bonded in a distorted single-bond geometry to two equivalent C(1) and one H(1) atom. The C(2)-H(1) bond length is 0.93 Å. H(1) is bonded in a single-bond geometry to one C(2) atom. The partial atomic charge ranges for C, Cu, H, O are -0.15 to 0.52, 0.83, 0.10 to 0.34, -0.75 to -0.27.
